creator: @soyenriquerocha
executive_summary: In a F&G=21 Extreme Fear environment with a structurally sound yield curve, the optimal moderate strategy is aggressive accumulation of high-quality oversold tech (NOW, SNOW, CRM, PLTR) anchored by defensive healthcare (GILD, LLY, AMGN) and a meaningful 20% materials allocation split between XAG mean-reversion and XAU safe-haven. The Hormuz blockade week 2 makes any materials reduction premature. The single highest-priority action today is establishing or adding to NOW before the 8-day earnings catalyst: PEG 0.854 and 4 consecutive beats at RSI 31.6 is a textbook reward-to-risk setup. Maintain 20% cash as both a hedge against Hormuz escalation and dry powder for post-earnings dips.

macro_environment.summary: The market sits in a contradictory but actionable regime: Extreme Fear (F&G=21) provides a contrarian accumulation signal while a positive yield spread (+0.657%) and contained VIX (18.24) confirm no recession or systemic panic. The Hormuz blockade sustains safe-haven demand for gold and silver, but ceasefire talks resuming introduces a binary oil event; the simultaneous tech selloff has created generational valuation dislocations in high-quality SaaS names (NOW PEG 0.854, CRM fwd PE 11.62) that are unrelated to geopolitical risk.
key_factors: Fear & Greed Index 21 = historically reliable contrarian buy signal for patient accumulators
Positive yield spread +0.657% rules out imminent recession — selloff is sentiment-driven, not structural
Hormuz blockade week 2 sustains XAU/XAG safe-haven bid; ceasefire resolution = oil binary event
S&P RSI 71.8 overbought at index level but dozens of individual names remain deeply oversold

cross_sector_insights.insight: Crypto rallying (ETH conf 8, SOL best RSI 54.8) while tech is deeply oversold (NOW RSI 31.6, SNOW RSI 30.4) signals a liquidity rotation, not a deleveraging event.
cross_sector_insights.implication: Deploy into oversold quality tech aggressively; crypto allocation additive. ETH and SOL offer asymmetric upside with institutional catalysts (JPMorgan on-chain repo, SOL ETFs $1B AUM).

cross_sector_insights.insight: Gold +47.9% YTD, silver +144.5% YTD, but gold/silver ratio still at 60.7x vs 47x historical mean — markets haven't priced the full industrial+monetary convergence in silver.
cross_sector_insights.implication: XAG is the highest-conviction materials trade: 34.9% below ATH with mean-reversion thesis and solar PV +38% YoY industrial tailwind intact.

cross_sector_insights.insight: Oil -6.35% on ceasefire hopes removes the primary inflation tail risk; confirmed Hormuz resolution is net-positive for tech valuations and crypto.
cross_sector_insights.implication: Hold COP only from existing position — redirect freed capital toward NOW, CRM, and ETH which benefit most from rate-stability re-rating.
